faqArr.name: What is course content and duration?
faqArr.text:
This course is divided into 4 major parts-
  • Basic Sciences as applied to psychiatry
  • Clinical Psychiatry
  • Psychiatry sub-specialties
  • Neurology and medicine as related to psychiatry
And, the complete duration of the course will be around 100+ hours.

faqArr.name: Who should join this course?
faqArr.text: The comprehensive study course encompassed the entire syllabus taught during 3 years of Psychiatry residency. It is beneficial for first, second, and third-year residents, as it offers thorough syllabus coverage for the entire three-year residency. It can also be used to create content for journal clubs and seminars.

faqArr.name: Which books are the course based on?
faqArr.text: The course is designed based on prominent textbooks, including Kaplan & Saddock, Synopsis, Rutter's Child & Adolescent Psychiatry, Lowenson and Ruiz Substance Abuse, Kauffman Neurology for Psychiatrists, Maudsley prescribing Guidelines, Stahl Psychopharmacology, SIMS, FISH, DSM-5, ICD11.
